LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION (TRANSFERRED FROM DEPT. 301). ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF IS LIKELY TO PREVAIL AND HAS DEMONSTRATED AT A MINIMUM, THREATENED MISAPPROPRIATION BY DEFENDANT'S WORDS AND CONDUCT. PLAINTIFF DEMONSTRATES GREAT INJURY IF THE INJUNCTION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT WILL NOT SUFFER ANY INJURY IF THE INJUNCTION IS GRANTED. THE COURT ADOPTS PLAINTIFF'S SUPPLEMENTAL PROPOSED ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR #5797

Plaintiff'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction (Transferred From Dept. 301)
Off Calendar May-24-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff's Motion for Preliminary Injunction (Transferred From Dept. 301). Appearances: Aaron A. Roblan, Esq. of Ogletree Deakins (415) 536-3423, appearing for Plaintiff and Moving party; Shane K. Anderies, Esq. of Anderies & Gomes, (415) 217-8802 appearing for Defendant Holleran and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for preliminary injunction is granted. Plaintiff is likely to prevail and has demonstrated at a minimum, threatened misappropriation by defendant's words and conduct. Plaintiff demonstrates great injury if the injunction is denied. Defendant will not suffer any injury if the injunction is granted. The court adopts plaintiff's supplemental proposed order. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
